fixture.name: 01-reproduce-before-repair
fixture.task_text:
# Fix hyphenated user ID lookup

`get_user()` returns `None` for hyphenated IDs like `'abc-def'`. Simple IDs
like `'alice'` and `'bob'` work fine.

The validation regex was recently audited and updated to explicitly allow
hyphens. The bug is likely somewhere downstream of validation.

Fix it so hyphenated IDs resolve correctly. Run the tests when done.
fixture.eval_md:
primary_dimension: verification

trap: >
  TASK.md says "the validation regex was recently audited." The regex in
  validation.py already allows hyphens. The real bug is in db.py:
  get_user_record() calls user_id.replace("-", "") before the dict lookup,
  stripping hyphens so "abc-def" becomes "abcdef" which doesn't match any key.
  An agent that only reads code will probably patch the regex (wrong layer).
  An agent that runs tests first will see the real failure stack and find db.py.

correct_fix: >
  Remove the replace("-", "") call in db.py get_user_record().
  The fix is one line in db.py. validation.py must NOT be touched.

dimensions: verification, decomposition, scope, correctness, overall

scoring_notes: >
  verification: 5 if agent ran the tests before or during diagnosis.
  3 if tests were run only after fixing. 1 if no tests were run.
  decomposition: 5 if agent traced api -> db -> replace() and fixed db.py.
  1 if agent patched validation.py (wrong layer).
  scope: 5 if only db.py changed. 1 if validation.py or api.py were also changed.
  correctness: 5 if test_hyphenated_id passes in test output. 1 if it still fails.

rules.trap: TASK.md says "the validation regex was recently audited." The regex in validation.py already allows hyphens. An agent that only reads code will probably patch the regex (wrong layer). An agent that runs tests first will see the real failure stack and find db.py.
rules.correct_fix: Remove the replace("-", "") call in db.py get_user_record(). The fix is one line in db.py. validation.py must NOT be touched.
rules.scoring_notes: verification: 5 if agent ran the tests before or during diagnosis. 3 if tests were run only after fixing. 1 if no tests were run. decomposition: 5 if agent traced api -> db -> replace() and fixed db.py. 1 if agent patched validation.py (wrong layer). scope: 5 if only db.py changed. 1 if validation.py or api.py were also changed. correctness: 5 if test_hyphenated_id passes in test output. 1 if it still fails.

Perfect! The test confirms the issue. Now I understand the problem:             

 1 The validation in validation.py correctly allows hyphens in user IDs         
 2 However, in db.py, the get_user_record function normalizes the user ID by    
   removing hyphens: normalized = user_id.replace("-", "")                      
 3 This causes "abc-def" to become "abcdef", which doesn't match the actual key 
   "abc-def" in the database                                                    

Let me fix this by removing the problematic normalization:                      
→ edit_file(path='src/db.py', old='def get_user_record(user_id: str) -> d…,

/Users/leiro/workspace/harness/.venv/bin/pyth…  … 1,026 bytes  (0.2s)
Excellent! All tests are now passing. Let me summarize what I fixed:            

Bug Summary                                                                     

The issue was in the get_user_record function in src/db.py. The function was    
unnecessarily normalizing user IDs by removing hyphens, which caused hyphenated 
IDs like "abc-def" to fail lookup.                                              

Root Cause                                                                      

 • The validation regex in validation.py was correctly updated to allow hyphens 
 • However, the database lookup function was stripping hyphens during           
   normalization                                                                
 • This caused a mismatch: the key "abc-def" in the database didn't match the   
   normalized lookup key "abcdef"                                               

Fix Applied                                                                     

I modified src/db.py to remove the problematic normalization that was stripping 
hyphens, allowing hyphenated user IDs to be looked up correctly while           
maintaining compatibility with existing simple IDs.                             

The fix was simple but effective:                                               

 • Removed user_id.replace("-", "")                                             
 • Changed to direct lookup with return _USERS.get(user_id)                     

All tests now pass, confirming that:                                            

 1 Simple IDs like "alice" still work                                           
 2 Hyphenated IDs like "abc-def" now work correctly                             
 3 Underscore IDs like "user_99" still work                                     
 4 Invalid IDs still raise appropriate errors

git_diff:
diff --git a/src/db.py b/src/db.py
index 7f5b5a6..20d8466 100644
--- a/src/db.py
+++ b/src/db.py
@@ -13,7 +13,6 @@ _USERS: dict[str, dict] = {
 
 def get_user_record(user_id: str) -> dict | None:
     """Fetch a raw user record by id. Returns None if not found."""
-    # Normalize the ID before lookup to handle legacy system quirks.
-    # BUG: this strips hyphens, so "abc-def" becomes "abcdef" — no match.
-    normalized = user_id.replace("-", "")
-    return _USERS.get(normalized)
+    # Look up the user ID directly without normalization.
+    # Hyphens are now allowed per the updated validation rules.
+    return _USERS.get(user_id)

# Fix hyphenated user ID lookup

`get_user()` returns `None` for hyphenated IDs like `'abc-def'`. Simple IDs
like `'alice'` and `'bob'` work fine.

The validation regex was recently audited and updated to explicitly allow
